surface.name: Rootly quick start from signup through a first Slack test incident
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Rootly's own Quick Start statement that Dashboard > Create Test Incident is the fastest first workflow makes the web route primary. It avoids the optional Slack installation and its admin permissions; signup is one opaque step at the official URL.

alternatives_considered.name: Dashboard Create Test Incident
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quick start calls this the fastest dashboard route, but its detailed setup spine still includes connecting Slack and does not document the dashboard modal field sequence on that page.

alternatives_considered.name: Microsoft Teams integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quick-start navigation presents Microsoft Teams as a peer communication surface with different installation, permission, and channel actions.

alternatives_considered.name: Google Chat integration
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quick-start navigation presents Google Chat as another peer communication surface rather than selecting Slack for every organization.

alternatives_considered.name: Rootly web or API incident creation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The docs home lists Slack, web, and API incident creation as alternatives, while the selected candidate follows the quick start's connected-Slack path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Rootly account, connect a standard Slack workspace, create a first test incident, and observe its dedicated incident channel and initial system message.
assumed_prior_state: No Rootly account, organization membership, Slack integration, connected Slack user, team configuration, or incident is assumed.
A Slack Free, Pro, or Business+ workspace and Slack Workspace Admin or Owner access are available for the otherwise documented downstream route.
A work email and password would be used for the documented signup route; no SSO-enforced domain or prior organization invitation is assumed.
No account, agreement, Slack authorization, integration, channel, or incident was created during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A test incident is created in Rootly's web app and the browser redirects to its incident page.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The newly created incident page opens with the incident record, lifecycle timestamp, and initial timeline entry.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Redirect you to the newly created incident page

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The redirected incident page is the earliest observable proof that Rootly accepted and created the first test incident.

prerequisites.requirement: Choose Google, Slack, SSO, email and password, or an organization invitation for Rootly identity; the candidate uses email and password.
The email-and-password route requires acceptance of Rootly's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
The standard Slack installation requires a Rootly account with Admin or Owner permissions and a Slack Workspace Admin or Owner account.
Choose the Slack Free, Pro, or Business+ workspace install or the Enterprise Grid organization install; the candidate uses a standard workspace install.
Slack must allow the Rootly app, including the scopes required for slash commands, messages, and public or private incident-channel creation.

primary_path.action: Open Rootly Quick Start and choose the dashboard test-incident route.
details: Rootly explicitly calls Create Test Incident the fastest first workflow.
primary_path.output: The fastest documented route is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The Quick Start identifies the dashboard action.

primary_path.action: Sign up at the official Rootly signup URL.
details: Treat signup as one opaque step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Rootly account and organization are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any identity verification or organization invitation required by the selected signup method.

primary_path.action: Open Dashboard and select Create Test Incident, or open Create Incident and enable Test Incident when available.
details: The normal web form can be opened from Dashboard, Incidents, or the global action menu.
primary_path.output: The New Incident form opens in test mode.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the documented Create Incident entry if the dashboard labels differ.

details: Default fields include title, summary, severity, type, and privacy; workspace-required fields are marked and enforced.
primary_path.output: The form is valid for submission.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Fill highlighted required or conditional fields.

primary_path.output: Rootly creates the incident and redirects.
primary_path.success_signal: The newly created incident page opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check missing permissions or private-incident restrictions.

primary_path.action: Verify the incident record and initial timeline entry on the incident page.
primary_path.output: A usable Rootly test incident exists.
primary_path.success_signal: The incident page shows the record and creation state.

branches.condition: The user chooses Google or Slack identity
branches.path: Authorize the selected third-party identity instead of creating a local password.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes credential creation, consent, and external-account dependencies.

branches.condition: The email domain is configured for SSO
branches.path: Follow the redirect to the organization's sign-in page and authenticate under its identity policy.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces self-service local signup with organization-controlled authentication.

branches.condition: The user was invited to an existing Rootly organization
branches.path: Open the invitation email and complete setup with one of the identity methods allowed by that organization.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds email invitation handling and assigns organization-controlled roles instead of creating a standalone account state.

branches.condition: The organization uses Slack Enterprise Grid
branches.path: Install at organization level as Org Owner or Admin, add Rootly to selected workspaces, choose incident, announcement, and alerts workspaces or channels, save, and optionally create shared channels.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds organization-level permission, workspace rollout, configuration, and possible shared-channel actions.

branches.condition: The user chooses the dashboard's Create Test Incident
branches.path: Use the dashboard control described by the quick start rather than the Slack command.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids the Slack command interaction but the public quick start does not enumerate every dashboard form field.

branches.condition: The user creates a live incident rather than a test
branches.path: Run `/rootly new` or create from a Slack message, complete the form, and select Create.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The incident can be publicly announced and can trigger configured workflows, notifications, tickets, checklists, and other real-process side effects.

friction_gates.description: Google, Slack, SSO, email and password, and invitation routes change identity and organization-entry actions.
The signup guide does not establish that a fresh self-service account has the Rootly Admin permission required by the Slack installation guide.
Slack setup requires Rootly Admin or Owner and Slack Workspace Admin or Owner permissions; Enterprise Grid requires an Org Owner or Admin.
Standard Slack, Enterprise Grid, Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, dashboard, web, and API routes have different setup and incident-creation actions.
Slack channel-creation restrictions can require additional admin scopes before Rootly can create incident channels.
The incident modal can enforce customized required, conditional, and lifecycle fields before Create is allowed.

time_to_first_success.value: about 15 minutes from signup to the first incident
